weather_report:
Subiaco sits under a quilt of scattered cloud, the late-morning sun slipping in and out, bright one moment and softened the next. The air looks clear and easy on the eyes, the sky a patchwork of blue and pale gray. Even with the veiled sunshine, midday UV can still bite, so take a hat, sunglasses, and a little sunscreen if you’ll be out and about.

Through the afternoon the pattern holds: sunny breaks, passing patches of shade, no great drama—just a pleasant, workable day. Dress light with a layer you can pocket, comfortable shoes for wandering, and maybe a brimmed hat for the midday stretch. It’s a fine time to walk your errands instead of driving, sit with a late coffee on a quiet veranda, hang the washing to catch the sun between clouds, or drift through a bookshop without a clock in your head. If work calls, open a window and let the bright, changeable light keep you company.
